message:
fix(tls): CertificatePinSet::validate() no longer fails unconditionally

compute_spki_sha256() always returns Err("Not implemented") since
x509_parser was disabled. The previous code used `?` to propagate this
error, causing ALL pin validation to fail — even cert-sha256-only sets.

Changed both compute calls to `.ok()` and match against Option, so
validation succeeds for pin types whose computation is available (e.g.
cert-sha256 via ring) while gracefully degrading for unimplemented types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
